archetype: Nuclear Power Plant Cooling System Monitoring and Control
description: A software system that monitors and controls the cooling systems of a nuclear power plant, ensuring safe operation and preventing overheating. Used by nuclear power plant operators, this system must adhere to strict safety protocols and regulations. Technically challenging due to the need for real-time data processing, high reliability, and adherence to nuclear industry standards.
rules: MUST integrate with existing SCADA systems for real-time data acquisition
MUST adhere to IAEA safety standards for nuclear power plant operations
MUST implement automatic fail-safe mechanisms to prevent overheating
MUST provide real-time alerts and notifications to operators in case of anomalies
MUST ensure data integrity and security in accordance with NRC regulations
anti_patterns: DO NOT use off-the-shelf solutions without customization for nuclear industry specificities
DO NOT neglect to implement redundancy in critical system components
DO NOT overlook the need for regular software updates and patching
key_technical_challenges: Real-time data processing and analysis of complex sensor data
Ensuring high reliability and fault tolerance in a safety-critical system
Implementing secure communication protocols for data transmission

recommended_stack.language: C++ for its performance, reliability, and compatibility with existing SCADA systems
recommended_stack.framework: Qt for its real-time capabilities and cross-platform support
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL for its reliability, security, and support for complex data types
recommended_stack.cache: Redis for its high performance and real-time data processing capabilities
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka for its fault-tolerant and scalable messaging capabilities
